title: Setup Python Environment and Dependencies
description: Configure virtual environment and install all required packages for SAE-GUIDE experiments. Ensure compatibility with CUDA for A6000 GPU.

steps.step1: Create/activate virtual environment: python -m venv .venv && source .venv/bin/activate
steps.step2: Install PyTorch 2.1+ with CUDA 12.1: pip install torch==2.1.2 torchvision --index-url https://download.pytorch.org/whl/cu121
steps.step4: Install sae-lens for SAE training: pip install sae-lens==1.0.0
steps.step5: Install datasets and evaluation: pip install datasets==2.14.0 evaluate==0.4.1
steps.step6: Install retrieval libraries: pip install rank-bm25==0.2.2 faiss-gpu==1.7.4 sentence-transformers==2.2.2
steps.step7: Install utilities: pip install numpy==1.24.0 scikit-learn==1.3.0 scipy==1.11.0 matplotlib==3.7.0 seaborn==0.12.0 pandas==2.0.0 tqdm
steps.step8: Verify GPU availability: python -c 'import torch; print(torch.cuda.get_device_name(0))'
steps.step9: Set random seeds for reproducibility: RANDOM_SEEDS=[42, 123, 456] to be used across all experiments

title: Download and Preprocess Multi-Hop QA Datasets
description: Download HotpotQA and 2WikiMultiHopQA dev sets. Extract reasoning chains and create train/val/test splits. Build BM25 retrieval index.

steps.step1: Download HotpotQA dev set (distractor setting): wget http://curtis.ml.cmu.edu/datasets/hotpot/hotpot_dev_distractor_v1.json
steps.step2: Sample 500 questions from HotpotQA dev for experiments (stratified by question type: bridge/comparison)
steps.step3: Download 2WikiMultiHopQA dev set: use HuggingFace datasets - datasets.load_dataset('2WikiMultiHopQA', 'dev')
steps.step5: Split each dataset: 60% train (480), 20% val (160), 20% test (160) - total 800 questions
steps.step6: Extract reasoning chains using Qwen2.5-7B with CoT prompt: 'Answer step by step'
steps.step7: Identify bridge entities and intermediate answers for each question using string matching heuristics
steps.step8: Build BM25 index over Wikipedia passages: use rank_bm25 with default k1=1.5, b=0.75
steps.step9: Create ground-truth retrieval labels: mark passage as relevant if contains answer or bridge entity
steps.step10: Save preprocessed data to data/processed/ with pickle format for fast loading

title: Implement Standard RAG Baseline
description: Implement simple single-retrieval RAG baseline. Retrieves once using the original question, then generates answer.

steps.step1: Load Qwen2.5-7B-Instruct model with bfloat16 precision: model = AutoModelForCausalLM.from_pretrained('Qwen/Qwen2.5-7B-Instruct', torch_dtype=torch.bfloat16, device_map='auto')
steps.step2: Implement retrieval function: retrieve(query, k=3) returns top-k BM25 passages
steps.step4: For each test question: retrieve with original question, generate answer with greedy decoding (temperature=0)
steps.step5: Compute metrics: Exact Match (EM), F1 score, Retrieval Precision@3
steps.step6: Run with 3 random seeds [42, 123, 456] - same model, different question ordering for confidence intervals
steps.step7: Expected: ~35-40% EM on HotpotQA (standard RAG struggles with multi-hop)

title: Implement IRCOT Periodic Retrieval Baseline
description: Implement IRCOT-style periodic retrieval. Retrieves every N=50 tokens during generation. This is a strong baseline for multi-hop reasoning.

steps.step1: Implement streaming generation with token counting: generate up to 50 tokens, then trigger retrieval
steps.step2: Construct retrieval query from generated text: use last 30 tokens as query
steps.step3: Append retrieved documents to context and continue generation
steps.step4: Set max retrieval rounds = 5 to prevent infinite loops
steps.step5: Compute metrics: EM, F1, Retrieval Precision@3, Average retrieval calls per question
steps.step7: Expected: ~45-50% EM, ~3-4 retrieval calls per question

title: Implement Probing-RAG Binary Probe Baseline
description: Implement Probing-RAG style binary classifier on hidden states to decide whether to retrieve. This is our strongest baseline and main comparison point.

steps.step1: Extract hidden states from layer 16 of Qwen2.5-7B during reasoning on training set (480 questions)
steps.step2: Label training examples: positive if next reasoning step needs retrieval (ground-truth bridge entity not in context)
steps.step3: Train binary MLP probe: input=3584-dim hidden state, hidden=512, output=1 (sigmoid)
steps.step4: Training config: lr=1e-4, batch_size=32, epochs=10, optimizer=AdamW with weight_decay=0.01
steps.step5: At inference: generate reasoning step-by-step, extract hidden state, probe predicts retrieval necessity
steps.step6: If probe score > 0.5, retrieve using original question + generated text
steps.step7: Compute metrics: EM, F1, Retrieval Precision@3, Detection Accuracy (% correct retrieval decisions)
steps.step8: Run with 3 random seeds for probe initialization [42, 123, 456]
steps.step9: Expected: ~48-52% EM, ~2-3 retrieval calls per question
steps.step10: Save predictions to results/probing_rag_predictions.json and probe weights to checkpoints/probing_rag/

title: SAE Training on Multi-Hop Reasoning Hidden States
description: Train Sparse Autoencoder on hidden states from intermediate layers during multi-hop reasoning. This is the foundation of SAE-GUIDE.

steps.step1: Generate hidden state dataset: Run Qwen2.5-7B on 480 training questions, extract hidden states from layers 12-20 at each reasoning step
steps.step2: Aggregate hidden states: pool across layers 12-20 using mean pooling, resulting in ~2K-5K state vectors (3584-dim each)
steps.step3: Initialize SAE: expansion_factor=8, input_dim=3584, hidden_dim=28672, top_k=32 (TopK SAE)
steps.step4: SAE architecture: encoder (3584 -> 28672 with ReLU), decoder (28672 -> 3584)
steps.step5: Training config: lr=1e-3, batch_size=256, epochs=100, loss = MSE_reconstruction + 0.1 * L1_sparsity
steps.step6: Early stopping: stop if validation reconstruction loss doesn't improve for 10 epochs
steps.step7: Expected training time: ~45-60 minutes on A6000 for ~3K hidden states
steps.step9: Evaluate reconstruction: target MSE < 0.01, average active features per input ~30-35
steps.step10: Run with 3 random seeds for SAE initialization [42, 123, 456]

title: Feature Annotation and Information-Need Mapping
description: Analyze SAE features to identify those correlated with information gaps. Create feature-to-query mapping for targeted retrieval. Addresses feedback on concrete mapping examples.

steps.step1: Extract SAE features for all training hidden states: z = SAE.encode(h), shape (N, 28672)
steps.step2: For each feature j (0 to 28671), collect top-50 activating examples
steps.step3: Analyze feature patterns: for each feature, examine context of activating examples
steps.step4: Classify features into types: entity_features (activate when entity info needed), temporal_features (dates/times), relation_features (relationships needed), other
steps.step5: Identify information-gap features: features with high activation when ground-truth intermediate answer is NOT in context
steps.step6: Build feature-to-query mapping dictionary: feature_type -> query_template
steps.step7: Entity feature template: extract entities from context, append 'information about {entity}'
steps.step8: Temporal feature template: append 'date timeline when' to query
steps.step9: Relation feature template: extract entity pair, append '{entity1} {entity2} relationship'
steps.step11: Count interpretable features: target >50% of top-100 active features have clear semantic meaning

title: Train Information-Need Probe on SAE Features
description: Train lightweight probe on cumulative SAE feature activations to predict when information is needed. Uses cumulative activation tracking.

steps.step1: Create training data: for each reasoning step, compute cumulative SAE feature activation with decay α=0.9
steps.step2: Cumulative formula: cumsum[t] = α * cumsum[t-1] + z_t, where z_t = SAE.encode(h_t)
steps.step3: Label training examples: positive if retrieval needed at this step (ground-truth label)
steps.step4: Train probe: 2-layer MLP, input=28672 (cumulative features), hidden=1024, output=1
steps.step5: Training config: lr=1e-4, batch_size=64, epochs=20, optimizer=AdamW, weight_decay=0.01
steps.step6: Loss: binary cross-entropy with class weighting (handle class imbalance)
steps.step7: Validation: tune threshold θ_retrieval on validation set to maximize F1

title: SAE-GUIDE Full System Evaluation
description: Evaluate complete SAE-GUIDE system with SAE-based information-need detection and feature-guided retrieval. Main results for paper.

steps.step1: Load trained SAE and probe from checkpoints
steps.step2: For each test question, initialize: context=empty, cumulative_features=zeros
steps.step3: While answer not generated: generate next reasoning step (max 30 tokens)
steps.step4: Extract hidden state h_t from layer 16, compute z_t = SAE.encode(h_t)
steps.step7: If uncertainty > θ_retrieval (tuned threshold): identify top-5 activated features
steps.step8: Generate augmented query: original_question + ' ' + feature_to_query(top_features, context)
steps.step9: Retrieve k=3 documents using augmented query, append to context
steps.step11: Compute metrics: EM, F1, Retrieval Precision@3, Average retrieval calls per question
steps.step12: Record feature activation statistics: which features activate, at what steps

title: Ablation: Binary Probe vs SAE-Based Probe
description: Ablation study comparing SAE-based probe against equivalent binary probe without SAE decomposition. Tests if SAE adds value.

steps.step1: Train binary probe directly on hidden states (same architecture as Probing-RAG, but same training data as SAE-GUIDE)
steps.step2: Compare: (a) Binary probe on raw hidden states vs (b) Probe on SAE features
steps.step4: Evaluate both on same test set with 3 seeds
steps.step5: Metrics: EM, F1, Detection Accuracy (% correct retrieval decisions)
steps.step6: Success criterion: SAE-based probe outperforms binary probe by >2% F1
steps.step7: If SAE doesn't help, this refutes core claim (R1 in proposal)

title: Ablation: Cumulative vs Per-Step Activation
description: Test whether cumulative feature activation provides benefit over per-step activation alone.

steps.step1: Train variant probe using only current step z_t (no cumulative sum)
steps.step2: Compare against cumulative version: cumulative = 0.9 * prev + z_t
steps.step3: Both use same SAE and feature-to-query mapping
steps.step4: Evaluate on test set with 3 seeds
steps.step6: Success criterion: Cumulative activation outperforms per-step by >1% F1
steps.step7: If no benefit, this is failure mode F3 - simplify to per-step

title: Ablation: With vs Without Feature-to-Query Mapping
description: Test whether feature-guided query augmentation improves retrieval quality over using original query alone.

steps.step1: Run SAE-GUIDE with feature-to-query mapping (full system)
steps.step2: Run SAE-GUIDE without mapping: always use original question for retrieval
steps.step3: Both use same SAE and probe for triggering
steps.step4: Compare Retrieval Precision@3: does feature-guided retrieval get better documents?
steps.step5: Compare EM/F1: does better retrieval lead to better answers?
steps.step6: Success criterion: Feature-to-query mapping improves Precision@3 by >5%
steps.step7: If no improvement, this is failure mode F4

title: Ablation: Layer Selection Analysis
description: Compare SAEs trained on different layer ranges to find optimal hidden state location for information-need detection.

steps.step1: Extract hidden states from early layers (4-8), mid layers (12-20), late layers (24-30)
steps.step2: Train 3 separate SAEs (one per layer range) with same hyperparameters
steps.step3: Train corresponding probes for each SAE
steps.step4: Evaluate all 3 variants on validation set
steps.step6: Report which layer range works best for information-need detection
steps.step7: Time budget: this adds ~30 minutes for 2 additional SAEs (parallel training possible)

title: Ablation: Feature Type Analysis
description: Test contribution of different feature types (entity, temporal, relation) to retrieval performance.

steps.step1: Run SAE-GUIDE using only entity-type features for query augmentation
steps.step2: Run SAE-GUIDE using only temporal-type features
steps.step3: Run SAE-GUIDE using only relation-type features
steps.step4: Run SAE-GUIDE using all feature types (full system)
steps.step5: Compare EM, F1, and Retrieval Precision across variants
steps.step6: Identify which feature types are most important for each dataset

title: Failure Mode Analysis
description: Systematically analyze failure modes F1-F5 as described in proposal. Categorize errors and analyze their prevalence.

steps.step1: F1 Analysis (Non-interpretable features): Sample top-100 most active features, use GPT-4 to generate descriptions, manual rating of meaningfulness
steps.step2: F2 Analysis (Features don't correlate with needs): Compute correlation between feature activation and ground-truth information needs, target r > 0.3
steps.step3: F3 Analysis (Cumulative no benefit): Compare per-step vs cumulative (from ablation), if no difference, F3 confirmed
steps.step4: F4 Analysis (Mapping fails): Compare retrieval precision with/without feature-to-query mapping, if no difference, F4 confirmed
steps.step5: F5 Analysis (Overfitting): Compare HotpotQA vs 2WikiMultiHopQA performance gap, gap >10% suggests overfitting
steps.step6: Categorize each error example into: wrong retrieval trigger, wrong retrieval query, correct retrieval but wrong answer, other
steps.step7: Report failure mode distribution across all test examples

title: Statistical Significance Testing
description: Conduct statistical tests to validate that SAE-GUIDE improvements are significant. Required for confirmation criteria.

steps.step1: Collect results from 3 random seeds for SAE-GUIDE and each baseline
steps.step2: Compute mean and standard deviation for EM and F1 per method
steps.step3: Perform paired t-test between SAE-GUIDE and Probing-RAG: target p < 0.05
steps.step4: Perform paired t-test between SAE-GUIDE and IRCOT: target p < 0.05
steps.step5: Compute effect size (Cohen's d) for significant differences
steps.step6: Report 95% confidence intervals for all metrics

title: Feature Interpretability Evaluation
description: Evaluate whether SAE features capture meaningful semantic concepts. Address confirmation criterion C3.

steps.step1: For top-100 most frequently activated features, collect 50 top-activating examples each
steps.step2: Use GPT-4 to generate feature description from examples: 'Based on these contexts, what concept does this feature detect?'
steps.step3: Manual rating (automated with GPT-4 as proxy): Rate each description as 'clearly meaningful', 'somewhat meaningful', or 'unclear'
steps.step4: Compare against baseline: generate random descriptions for same features, rate them
steps.step5: Success criterion: >50% of features rated as 'clearly meaningful' or 'somewhat meaningful'
steps.step6: Report examples of best and worst feature interpretations

title: Generate Paper Figures and Tables
description: Create all visualizations and tables for the research paper.

steps.step1: Figure 1: Architecture diagram showing SAE-GUIDE pipeline (4 phases)
steps.step2: Figure 2: Example feature activation patterns during multi-hop reasoning (show cumulative activation over steps)
steps.step3: Figure 3: Comparison bar chart: EM and F1 across all methods (Standard RAG, IRCOT, Probing-RAG, SAE-GUIDE) with error bars
steps.step4: Figure 4: Retrieval efficiency plot: Accuracy vs Average retrieval calls per question
steps.step5: Figure 5: Ablation results: contribution of each component (binary vs SAE, cumulative vs per-step, with/without mapping)
steps.step6: Figure 6: Failure mode distribution pie chart
steps.step7: Table 1: Main results table - EM, F1, Precision@3, Retrieval calls for all methods across datasets
steps.step10: Save all figures to figures/ directory in PDF and PNG formats

title: Success Criteria Validation
description: Final validation against confirmation and refutation criteria from proposal.

steps.step1: Check C1: SAE-GUIDE significantly better than Probing-RAG (p < 0.05)?
steps.step2: Check C2: SAE-GUIDE reduces retrieval calls by 15%+ vs IRCOT with comparable accuracy?
steps.step3: Check C3: >50% of top features rated as interpretable?
steps.step4: Check C4: SAE features outperform binary probe in ablation?
steps.step5: Check R1 (refutation): If SAE-GUIDE not better than binary probe, claim is refuted
steps.step6: Check R2 (refutation): If interpretability <30%, features don't capture semantic needs
steps.step7: Check R3 (refutation): If cumulative no better than per-step
steps.step8: Check R4 (refutation): If feature-to-query mapping doesn't improve precision
steps.step9: Generate final verdict: CONFIRMED / PARTIALLY CONFIRMED / REFUTED with evidence
